Bay clinic is a medical centre in Brazil that operates with a unique mission of blending research and education with clinical and hospital care. The medical center has a huge head force of 25,000 employees, and as a result of the combined effort of those employees, the medical center has been able to handle approximately 3 million visits so far. In recent times, the hospital was incurring losses despite having the finest doctors available and not lacking scheduled appointments. To investigate the reason for the anomaly, a sample data dump of appointments 
medicalcentre.csv is hereby presented. The collected data provides information on the patient’s age, gender, 
appointment date, various diseases, etc. To cut costs, predict if a patient will show up on the appointment day or not (i.e., predict the appointment status) by completing the following: 
A. Data Wrangling & Feature Engineering (45 marks): 
1. Prepare the data for downstream processes, e.g., deal with missing values, duplicates 2. Determine the frequency of distinct values in each feature set 
3. Initialize a function to plot relevant features within the dataset to visualize for outliers 4. Count the frequency of negative Age feature observations, and remove them 
5. The values within AwaitingTime are negative, transform them into positive values 
6. ML algorithm requires the variables to be coded into its equivalent integer codes. Encode the string 
categorical values into an integer code
 
7. Separate the date features into date components 
8. ML algorithms work best when the input data are sc …[truncated]

I'm looking for some book recommendations. I recently joined a Facebook group called "Book Lovers Unite" three weeks ago and I've been loving the discussions and recommendations from the members. Can you suggest some popular books in the thriller genre?

I think "Gone Girl" would be a great choice for a book club discussion, especially since I've already been active in my Facebook group "Book Lovers Unite" and I think the members would really enjoy it. Do you think I could get some discussion questions from you to guide the conversation?

These discussion questions are really helpful! I think they'll definitely help guide the conversation and encourage the members in "Book Lovers Unite" to share their thoughts and opinions. I'm also thinking of asking the members to come prepared with their own questions and insights, so we can have a more dynamic discussion. Do you think it's a good idea to set a specific topic or theme for the discussion, like the portrayal of marriage or the impact of social media, to help focus the conversation?

I'm thinking of asking the members of "Book Lovers Unite" to come prepared to discuss the theme of marriage and relationships in "Gone Girl". I think this theme is really relevant to the book and will generate some interesting discussions. Do you think it's a good idea to also ask members to share their own experiences or thoughts about marriage and relationships in general, or would that be too personal?

I think I'll ask the members to come prepared to discuss the theme of marriage and relationships in "Gone Girl", and also encourage them to share their thoughts on how the book relates to their own beliefs or experiences with relationships. I'll make sure to establish a safe and respectful environment and set guidelines to keep the discussion focused on the book. Thanks for the advice!

I'm glad you're interested in my book! My favorite author is actually a mystery writer, and I've been following her series for years. I was thrilled to see her latest release was part of the 12 Days of Deals promotion, so I snapped it up right away.

As for staying organized, I have to say that the promotion did help me stay focused on what I needed to get. I had a list of people I needed to buy for, and I was able to check off a few gifts during the sale. Of course, I did end up browsing a bit and finding some other things I wanted, but overall, I think the promotion helped me stay on track and avoid impulse buys. Plus, it was nice to have some extra time to think about my purchases and make sure I was getting the best deals.

I'm looking for some book recommendations. I just finished reading the latest bestseller by John Grisham and loved it. Speaking of which, I attended a meetup organized by Book Lovers Unite last week where we discussed the book, and it was so much fun debating the plot twists and characters with fellow book lovers. Do you have any suggestions for my next read?

I think I'll start with "The Lincoln Lawyer" and see how I like it. I've heard great things about Michael Connelly's writing style, and I'm curious to see how he develops Mickey Haller's character. By the way, do you have any recommendations for book clubs or online forums where I can discuss the book with other readers? I just attended a meetup organized by a group last week where we discussed the latest bestseller by John Grisham, and it was so much fun debating the plot twists and characters with fellow book lovers.

I'm excited to dive into "The Lincoln Lawyer" and explore the world of Mickey Haller. I think I'll start with Goodreads and join some groups dedicated to Michael Connelly and legal thrillers. It's great to know that there are so many online communities and book clubs where I can discuss the book with other readers. Thanks for the recommendations!

I'll start by joining some Goodreads groups dedicated to Michael Connelly and legal thrillers. I'll also check out the Michael Connelly Fans Facebook group to see what discussions are happening there. Do you think I should create a post introducing myself and mentioning that I just finished reading the latest bestseller by John Grisham and attended a meetup organized by a group last week, where we discussed the book? That way, I can connect with other readers who might have similar interests.

I've been meaning to read more thrillers, and these recommendations are really helpful. I've heard of some of these authors before, but haven't gotten around to reading their books yet. Do you think any of these would be good for a book club discussion?

I'm intrigued by the legal thrillers, especially Michael Connelly's "The Lincoln Lawyer". I've heard great things about his writing style. Can you tell me more about the book's plot and the main character, Mickey Haller?
